Program Overview
Approved by the National Healthcareer
Association (NHA) · SCHEV Certified

Skilled with every draw

As a phlebotomy technician, you'll draw and collect blood specimens for laboratory testing — working in hospitals, clinics, labs and blood donor centers. Our program teaches the venipuncture skills and safety standards that matter on the job.

You'll learn anatomy of the circulatory system, infection control, the order of draw, specimen processing and patient communication — plus hands-on venipuncture practice through lab and simulation.

After finishing, you'll be ready to take the National Healthcareer Association (NHA) Certified Phlebotomy Technician (CPT) exam — a nationally recognized credential that opens more job options and room to advance.

Enroll Today
